content:
## Card Status Decision
For a dispute coded as recurring_charge_after_cancellation, do not close or replace the debit card solely because of that category. The required card action is keep_active, so leave the card usable unless a separate documented reason applies.

## Liability Treatment
Apply the limited-liability schedule only when the dispute category is one of unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud.

1. If the customer reports within 2 business days after discovery, cap liability at 50.
2. If the report comes after that period but still inside the statement window, cap liability at 500.
3. If the report comes after the statement window, record the exposure as unlimited liability in this benchmark policy.
4. For non-unauthorized error categories, customer liability is 0.

See "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" when the timing of discovery or statement-window status needs review.

## Provisional Credit Check
Recurring_charge_after_cancellation is not itself listed among categories that allow provisional credit. The allowed set is unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud, atm_cash_discrepancy, duplicate_charge. If the facts also support one of those categories, follow the provisional-credit handling for that category; otherwise do not promise provisional credit under this procedure. See "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy" for the credit decision rules.
title: Internal: Recurring Charge After Cancellation Handling
